title: LayerSelect: Attention-Aware Multi-Objective Hub Selection for Cross-Layer KV Cache Sharing
description: A training-free framework that selects optimal hub layers for cross-layer KV cache sharing using multi-objective optimization. Unlike existing methods that rely solely on KV cache similarity (xKV, MiniCache) or fixed patterns (CLA), LayerSelect builds a Query-KV Dependency Graph to capture how queries actually use KV caches, then optimizes for coverage, diversity, and attention alignment simultaneously. This addresses the tension between similarity-based and dissimilarity-based sharing discovered by KVSharer.
motivation: Cross-layer KV cache sharing is essential for reducing memory in long-context LLM inference. Existing approaches have critical limitations: (1) CLA uses fixed uniform grouping ignoring heterogeneous layer needs; (2) xKV and MiniCache rely on KV-KV similarity (CKA/cosine) for compression, missing how queries actually use the caches; (3) KVSharer discovered that sharing dissimilar caches can work better, but uses simple search without principled optimization; (4) HShare uses greedy selection for token indices rather than KV values. We hypothesize that attention-aware hub selection balancing coverage and diversity will achieve better memory-accuracy tradeoffs.
proposed_approach: LayerSelect operates in three phases: (1) Build a Query-KV Dependency Graph by measuring cross-layer attention patterns during calibration; (2) Solve a multi-objective optimization (NSGA-II) to select hub layers that maximize coverage (serving spoke layer needs), maximize diversity (avoiding redundant hubs per KVSharer's finding), and minimize attention misalignment; (3) Deploy with attention-weighted dynamic routing where spoke layers aggregate hub KV caches based on learned dependency scores. This is training-free, requires only ~10 minutes of profiling, and works with existing pretrained models.

related_work.title: Reducing Transformer Key-Value Cache Size with Cross-Layer Attention
related_work.authors: William Brandon, Mayank Mishra, Aniruddha Nrusimha, Rameswar Panda, Jonathan Ragan-Kelley
related_work.key_contribution: Introduces architectural cross-layer attention requiring retraining; uses fixed uniform grouping of adjacent layers.
related_work.how_we_differ: LayerSelect is training-free and uses data-driven multi-objective optimization instead of fixed patterns.

related_work.title: xKV: Cross-Layer SVD for KV-Cache Compression
related_work.authors: Chi-Chih Chang, Chien-Yu Lin, Yash Akhauri, Wei-Cheng Lin, Kai-Chiang Wu, Luis Ceze, Mohamed S. Abdelfattah
related_work.key_contribution: Uses CKA to identify layers with aligned singular vectors, applies cross-layer SVD compression.
related_work.how_we_differ: xKV focuses on KV-KV representation similarity via CKA/SVD. LayerSelect uses Query-KV attention dependency and multi-objective optimization with attention-weighted routing—fundamentally different approach based on usage patterns.

related_work.title: MiniCache: KV Cache Compression in Depth Dimension for Large Language Models
related_work.authors: Akide Liu, Jing Liu, Zizheng Pan, Yefei He, Gholamreza Haffari, Bohan Zhuang
related_work.key_contribution: Merges adjacent layers using SLERP based on cosine similarity; training-free.
related_work.how_we_differ: MiniCache is limited to adjacent similar layers. LayerSelect can select any layers as hubs and balances diversity with coverage via multi-objective optimization.

related_work.title: KVSharer: Efficient Inference via Layer-Wise Dissimilar KV Cache Sharing
related_work.authors: Yifei Yang, Zouying Cao, Qiguang Chen, Libo Qin, Dongjie Yang, Hai Zhao, Zhi Chen
related_work.key_contribution: Discovers counterintuitive phenomenon: sharing dissimilar KV caches can better preserve performance; uses simple search strategy.
related_work.how_we_differ: KVSharer uses search for dissimilar pairs. LayerSelect explicitly encodes diversity as an objective in multi-objective optimization, providing principled tradeoff with coverage.

related_work.title: HShare: Fast LLM Decoding by Hierarchical Key-Value Sharing
related_work.authors: Huaijin Wu, Lianqiang Li, Hantao Huang, Yi Tu, Jihang Zhang, Minghui Yu, Junchi Yan
related_work.key_contribution: Shares critical token indices across layers/heads/queries using greedy algorithm; reduces which tokens are stored.
related_work.how_we_differ: HShare is complementary—reduces token indices while we reduce layer redundancy. Our multi-objective optimization is more principled than greedy selection.

related_work.title: You Only Cache Once: Decoder-Decoder Architectures for Language Models
related_work.authors: Zhenyu Sun, Hongkuan Zhou, Wenjie Qu, Yan Hu, Xinyang Zhang, Yingxia Shao, Xuequn Shang
related_work.key_contribution: YOCO uses global KV cache for top-half layers; requires architectural changes.
related_work.how_we_differ: YOCO requires retraining with new architecture. LayerSelect is plug-and-play for existing models.

hypothesis: Attention-aware multi-objective hub selection that balances coverage (information needs of spoke layers), diversity (informational uniqueness of hubs), and attention alignment will achieve better memory-accuracy tradeoffs than similarity-based methods (xKV, MiniCache), uniform grouping (CLA), or greedy selection (HShare). Specifically, at 2× compression, LayerSelect will achieve <1% accuracy drop on long-context benchmarks compared to 2-3% for baselines.

confirming: Achieves ≥2× compression with <2% accuracy drop on RULER long-context benchmark
Dominates baselines on accuracy-compression Pareto frontier
Ablation studies show each objective (coverage, diversity, alignment) contributes meaningfully
Attention distribution divergence is 30-50% lower than direct sharing methods
refuting: Query-KV dependency provides no benefit over KV-KV similarity alone
Multi-objective optimization does not outperform simple greedy selection
Attention-weighted routing introduces unacceptable latency overhead
Method fails to generalize across model architectures

computational_requirements.feasibility: Training-free approach requires only inference profiling. Models up to 8B-14B parameters fit within constraints. Long-context benchmarks (4K-16K) feasible with reduced batch sizes.

expected_deliverables: Implementation of LayerSelect with Query-KV Dependency Graph construction
Multi-objective optimization solver (NSGA-II) for hub selection
Comparison against CLA, MiniCache, xKV, KVSharer on RULER and LongBench
Analysis of Pareto frontier across compression ratios
key_innovations: Query-KV Dependency Graph: Captures actual usage patterns via cross-layer attention rather than just representation similarity
Multi-Objective Hub Selection: Explicitly balances coverage vs diversity (addressing KVSharer's finding) vs attention alignment
Attention-Weighted Dynamic Routing: Dynamic aggregation based on dependency scores rather than direct copy or SVD compression
novelty_claims: First to use Query-KV attention dependency (not just KV-KV similarity) for cross-layer sharing decisions
First to formulate cross-layer hub selection as multi-objective optimization with explicit diversity objective
First to use attention-weighted dynamic routing for cross-layer KV cache aggregation
Training-free and plug-and-play, unlike CLA/YOCO which require retraining
